nyheter.title: Norge lærer opp dansk luftvernpersonell
nyheter.summary: Denne våren fortsetter dansk-norsk samarbeid i Nord-Norge under øvelsen Cold Response. Danske soldater har reist til Evenes flystasjon for videre trening sammen med norske luftvernsoldater.

nyheter.title: NATO trekker personell ut av Irak
nyheter.summary: På grunn av den stadig forverrede sikkerhetssituasjonen i Midtøsten trekker NATO sitt personell fra Nato Mission Iraq (NMI) ut av landet. Også norsk personell knyttet til dette oppdraget er nå trukket ut av Irak.

nyheter.title: Norden sammen om felles flylegeutdanning
nyheter.summary: Norge, Sverige, Finland og Danmark går sammen om å utvikle en felles utdanning for militære flyleger. I dag sendes kandidatene til USA, men nå ønsker de nordiske landene å bygge opp et eget kurs som er bedre tilpasset nordiske forhold – samtidig som samarbeidet med USA videreføres.

nyheter.title: Status: Søk utdanning i Forsvaret – for Norge
nyheter.summary: Forsvaret har en rekke spennende og attraktive utdanninger. Og du gjør en meningsfull jobb for Norge. Nå nærmer søknadsfristen 15. april seg.

nyheter.title: Funn fra pilotprosjekt om arbeidstid­planlegging og lengre planhorisont
nyheter.summary: Bedre arbeidstidsplanlegging og lengre planhorisont gir økt forutsigbarhet for forsvarsansatte.

nyheter.title: Evakueringen av ukrainske pasienter fortsetter
nyheter.summary: Evakueringen av ukrainske pasienter fra krigen i Ukraina forsetter etter at avtalen med SAS om bruk av et medisinsk evakueringsfly nå er forlenget. Evakueringen skjer i samarbeid med EU etter ønske fra de.

nyheter.title: Regjeringen vil styrke Forsvarsløftet med 115 milliarder kroner
nyheter.summary: Regjeringen vil øke de økonomiske rammene for langtidsplanen for forsvarssektoren med ytterligere 115 milliarder kroner frem til 2036.

nyheter.title: Status: Oppdatert langtidsplan – hva betyr det?
nyheter.summary: I dag la regjeringen frem sitt forslag til oppdatert langtidsplan. Forsvarssjef Eirik Kristoffersen forteller om planen og veien videre.

nyheter.title: Logistikkens livline under Cold Response 2026
nyheter.summary: KNM «Maud» er Nordens største og eneste militære tankskip. Under årets storøvelse fikk fartøyet vist hvorfor hun omtales som en av Forsvarets viktigste kapasiteter.

nyheter.title: Eksotisk jobbsjanse: – Jeg er heldig som får være her
nyheter.summary: Hva tenker du om nordlys og mørketid, ishav-stemning og sterkt samhold? Nå er verdens mest eksotiske jobb ledig. – Dette har gitt meg mer enn jeg hadde trodd var mulig, sier en som har hatt jobben før.

nyheter.title: French Shipyard Launches Third Patrol Vessel for Belgian Navy
nyheter.summary:
On April 2, 2026, French shipbuilder SOCARENAM launched the Castor-class coastal patrol vessel BNS Vega (P-903) for the Belgian Navy. This is the third ship-in-class after Castor and Pollux, launched about a decade earlier. After launch, the vessel was floated out of the dry dock to commence the dockside fitting-out phase. During this stage, construction ...

nyheter.title: Pentagon’s New $65.8B Shipbuilding Request is Highest Since 1962
nyheter.summary: The Pentagon’s request for new shipbuilding funds is among the highest-ever since the Korean War, rivaling the Reagan-era 600-ship spending plan and the nuclear submarine buildup in the 1960s, according to budget documents. In a $1.5 trillion Fiscal Year 2027 national security submission split between the base budget and reconciliation, the Navy wants a total of $65.8 billion for shipbuilding, as President Donald Trump pursues several new ship classes under the Golden Fleet concept. nyheter.title: India Inducts Third SSBN, Fourth Nilgiri-class Frigate
nyheter.summary:
India’s third Arihant-class SSBN INS Aridhaman and fourth Nilgiri-class frigate INS Taragiri were commissioned on April 3 at Visakhapatnam by defence minister Rajnath Singh. Defence minister Rajnath Singh posted a cryptic tweet translating to “It’s not words but power, Aridhaman!”. Multiple Indian media confirmed that the submarine was inducted. However, there was no official confirmation ...

nyheter.title: France orders its fifth and final FDI frigate from Naval Group, completing fleet plan
nyheter.summary: The first frigate in the class, the Amiral Ronarc’h, was delivered in October, and is currently on a long-term deployment.

nyheter.title: Seoul Expands Sub Warfare Force with U.S. Helos, Patrol Aircraft
nyheter.summary: The South Korean Navy stood up its first American-made MH-60R Seahawk helicopters Wednesday in the service’s latest move to bolster Seoul’s submarine hunting forces. Ordered in a $878 million deal from 2020, 12 anti-submarine helicopters were procured under the Republic of Korea Navy’s (ROKN) Maritime Helicopter-II program. nyheter.title: Carrier USS Gerald R. Ford Departs Croatia After Liberty, Additional Repairs
nyheter.summary: USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) departed Split, Croatia, following a five-day port visit, the Navy announced Thursday. It is unclear where the aircraft carrier is headed. Ford has been out for 282 days and is set to have a record-breaking deployment. “Gerald R. Ford remains poised for full mission tasking in support of national objectives in any area of operation,” reads the Navy news release. The Gerald R. nyheter.title: French, Italian Carrier Strike Groups Wrap NATO’s Neptune Strike Drills
nyheter.summary: The French and Italian Navy carrier strike groups spent the past week carrying out maritime strikes and air patrols in the Mediterranean and Black seas as part of NATO’s Neptune Strike 26-1.  “From the flight deck of Charles De Gaulle, we have conducted several maritime strikes and air patrols above the Mediterranean and the Black Sea in order to strengthen the Alliance’s deterrent posture and reassure partners in the region. nyheter.title: A year after ‘Liberation Day,’ impact of Trump tariffs on defense industry remains muted
nyheter.summary: Experts and former defense officials said exemptions to the tariffs imposed by the Trump administration have somewhat blunted the financial impact on the defense industrial base.

The vessel, to be named Amiral Cabanier, will be built at the shipyard in Lorient. The order announcement was made today by the French Ministry of the Armed Forces. nyheter.title: Two Former Navy Fighter Pilots Lead NASA’s Artemis II Mission
nyheter.summary: Two former U.S. Navy fighter pilots are aboard the first crewed orbit around the moon in more than 50 years after launching on Wednesday from NASA’s Kennedy Space Center on the Artemis II mission. Retired Capt. Reid Wiseman is the commander of the Artemis II mission, while Capt. Victor Glover Jr. is the pilot. Wiseman and Glover are among two other astronauts – Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen, a Royal Canadian Air Force fighter pilot, and NASA flight engineer Christina Koch – who launched Wedne

nyheter.title: Japan Deploys New Hypersonic, Anti-ship Missiles
nyheter.summary: Tokyo’s first batch of long-range strike missiles capable of reaching ground and maritime targets at extended ranges have been operationally fielded, according to Japan’s defense ministry. The Japan Ground Self-Defense Force (JGSDF) announced Monday the deployment of the Type 25 series of missiles. While the missiles share the same designation, their variants are split through surface-to-ship (SSM) and hypervelocity gliding projectile (HGP) classifications. nyheter.title: Amphibious Warship USS Rushmore Sailing Through Strait of Malacca
nyheter.summary: Dock landing ship USS Rushmore (LSD-47) sailed through the Singapore Strait this week and is headed west through the Strait of Malacca, according to ship spotters. Rushmore, a Whidbey Island-class amphibious warship, is expected to meet up with the Tripoli Amphibious Ready Group in U.S. Central Command, USNI News previously reported. ST Engineering’s Marine business has secured a six-year sub-contract valued at about S$600 million from Abu Dhabi Ship Building (ADSB) to design and supply the platform systems for a fleet of eight Missile Gun Boats that ADSB is constructing for the Kuwait Naval Force. As the U.S. Navy debates the future of ‘Trump-class’ battleships, other navies are advancing their own visions of ultra-large surface combatants. Bush (CVN-77) orange while sailors on Pier 14 busied themselves preparing for the carrier to shove off across the Atlantic. The mood of families at the pier Tuesday was subdued without the pomp and cheer of last summer’s deployment of USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) – the last carrier to leave the East Coast. The JUMP 20-X enables multi-domain missions with fully autonomous, hands-free operation and more than 13 hours of flight time, a 115-mile (185 km) range, and 30 pounds of payload capacity.
